title: Pipelines to payloads: risk management lessons for the space economy
summary: The article draws parallels between risk management in the energy sector and the emerging space economy, highlighting the influence of sovereign power, geopolitics, regulation, and private capital. It suggests that the space industry can learn valuable lessons from the energy sector's decades of experience in managing complex disputes.

title: AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raises $29.5 million angel round
summary: Chinese AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raised $29.5 million in an angel funding round. The six-month-old company aims to use AI to accelerate rocket engine development.

title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary: Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords on July 17, becoming the 70th nation to join the international agreement in under six years. This move aligns the island nation with NASA's lunar exploration and sustainable space principles.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary: The U.S. Space Force increased the maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract from $5.6 billion to $17 billion to meet rising demand. This move reflects growing needs in national security space missions.

title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
summary: The Pentagon awarded $7.1 million to Martin Materials Solutions to expand U.S. production of radiation-resistant cover glass for satellites. The funding, provided under the Defense Production Act, supports spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit on its first launch on July 18, marking the first commercial Indian rocket to achieve this milestone.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA and Draper have ended their lunar lander mission due to major delays in spacecraft development. The termination marks a setback for planned lunar exploration efforts.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made it a significant player in capital markets, highlighting its vision of interplanetary civilization. The company's main challenge now is securing more facilities to support its growing operations.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has signed the Artemis Accords, joining the U.S.-led initiative for lunar exploration. This move comes over two years after Serbia also joined China’s lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 on July 16 due to some engines failing to ignite. This marks another delay in the vehicle's testing and development process.

title: The 1st 'Avengers: Doomsday' trailer is here, and it's stuffed to bursting with superheroes
summary: The first trailer for 'Avengers: Doomsday' has been released, featuring a large ensemble of superheroes and the debut of Doctor Doom.

title: SpaceX targets July 23 for crucial Starship Flight 13 test launch after abort
summary: SpaceX has rescheduled the Flight 13 test launch of its Starship rocket to July 23 after an abort at the last second on July 16. The company is aiming to proceed with the crucial test following the initial delay.

title: Scientists discover mystery molecule on Pluto and Saturn's moon Titan: 'We cannot say what it is'
summary: Scientists have identified a mysterious molecule on both Pluto and Saturn's moon Titan, despite their vast separation. The origin and nature of this shared surface signature remain unexplained.

title: 50 years after Viking 1's historic Mars landing, NASA is looking to the Red Planet's skies
summary: NASA is commemorating the 50th anniversary of Viking 1's Mars landing, which occurred on July 20, 1976. The agency is now focusing on future missions to explore the skies of Mars.

title: 'Stranger Things' at 10: The Netflix streaming icon that made it cool to like science again
summary: The article discusses the cultural impact of 'Stranger Things' on streaming and its role in sparking younger audiences' interest in science. It highlights the show's influence beyond entertainment.

title: Twice in a row: SpaceX rocket aborts launch at last second again (video)
summary: SpaceX experienced its second consecutive last-second abort during a Falcon 9 launch of Starlink satellites on July 20. The incident occurred just before liftoff, marking a rare back-to-back failure for the company.

title: Four astronauts, 3 spacecraft, 2 weeks in orbit: Artemis III will be NASA's most complicated mission ever
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will be the agency's most complex mission to date, involving four astronauts, three spacecraft, and a two-week stay in orbit. The mission will require the launch and rendezvous of multiple spacecraft.

title: Artemis II was an incredible reminder of our shared humanity just when we needed it most (op-ed)
summary: The Artemis II mission reminded humanity of its shared purpose and unity, with astronauts expressing honor and eagerness to return home. The op-ed highlights the mission's emotional and unifying impact.

title: Get stabilized views of the cosmos with the Canon 18x50 IS binoculars, now almost 30% off
summary: Canon 18x50 IS binoculars are now available at a 30% discount on Amazon, making them a great option for stargazing due to their 50mm objective lenses and image stabilization.

title: Ancient sandstorm leaves behind wreckage on Mars | Space photo of the day for July 20, 2026
summary: A sandstorm on Mars has uncovered ancient wreckage, as shown in a space photo from July 20, 2026. The remnants highlight historical debris scattered across the planet's surface.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new attempt of Starship Flight 13 after last week's aborted launch. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are scheduled for upcoming launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Three companies—Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space—are making progress on their new medium-to-heavy-lift launch vehicles: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5, respectively. While SpaceX's Starship has dominated recent headlines, these developments highlight growing competition in the reusable rocket sector.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition due to Booster 20 failing to ignite properly. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was postponed as a result.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for its first land recovery.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This mission marks another crewed flight to the orbiting laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Seven launches are scheduled worldwide this week, including the 13th flight test of SpaceX’s Starship and a Soyuz mission to launch a crew to the ISS.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step before its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test using 33 engines. This milestone brings SpaceX closer to its next major launch or testing phase.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: New launch pads are being constructed at Cape Canaveral for Stoke Space and Relativity Space as part of the Space Coast's ongoing revitalization. This expansion reflects growing activity among multiple launch providers in the region.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details about the news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past 50 years. This publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, informing industry about planned activities and opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange on implementation.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. The innovation is expected to streamline operations and reduce costs in the space industry.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to ensure spacecraft success.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace became the first private Indian company to reach orbit with its Vikram-1 rocket, lifting off from India’s Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its budget for national security space launches by $11.4 billion, raising the total contract ceiling from $5.6 billion to $17 billion. This move significantly expands the pool of available launch contracts under the National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs has raised $10 million in a seed funding round and relocated its operations to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has selected SpaceX's Starlink to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission. This contract highlights SpaceX's role in supporting lunar exploration efforts.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the organization's future following liquidation. The interview explores the aftermath and potential next steps for ISU.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has secured $70 million in Series C funding to expand its operations and meet the rising demand for sovereign space hardware. The investment will support the company's growth in the global space industry.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed mission authorization framework, questioning its financial feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns about the office's ability to manage the new framework.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: SpaceX's potential Falcon 9 sales freeze raises concerns about the reliability of launch services, impacting satellite businesses. The situation questions the future of many space companies that relied on SpaceX's proven launch model.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's nominee for space acquisition highlighted that legacy space programs were not designed for modern acquisition reforms reshaping the Space Force. Senators questioned the nominees on space policy during the hearing.

title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
summary: The UK government will invest an additional £62 million ($83.4 million) to strengthen the country's space sector, focusing on new satellite communications systems. This funding aims to enhance the UK's capabilities in the space industry.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully conducted India's first private orbital launch with its Vikram-1 rocket, marking a historic milestone for the country's space industry.

title: ThinKom ThinAir GT Antenna Secures Government Certification for O3b mPower
summary: ThinKom's ThinAir GT2517 terminal has become the first airborne antenna to receive SES's Government Technology Certification for use on the O3b mPOWER constellation. This milestone enables government applications on the high-throughput satellite network.

title: Spire Hires Mantech Exec Eric Mellinger as COO
summary: Spire Global has appointed Eric Mellinger, a former Mantech International executive, as its Chief Operating Officer to oversee business development and go-to-market strategy. Mellinger brings nearly eight years of experience from Mantech International to his new role.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on lunar exploration. The partnership involves exchanging technical information and conducting system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline developed with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness. This marks the company’s first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes developing space-based moving target indication systems and disrupting Chinese military space systems as key deterrence capabilities. He emphasizes these as top priorities for the Space Force's future.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the emerging trend of space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit. Industry players debated the feasibility and potential advantages of orbital compute stacks.
